card.intuition: Assign each node a discovery time (disc) and a lowlink value (low) = the smallest disc reachable from its subtree via back-edges. When low[u] == disc[u], node u is the root of an SCC — pop the stack until u to collect the component.
card.analogy: Finding tightly-knit social circles: track when each person joined the network (disc) and how far back their connections reach (low). When someone's reach doesn't extend before they arrived, they and their followers form a closed clique.
card.space_complexity: O(V) for stack and disc/low arrays
when_to_use: Finding strongly connected components in a directed graph
Condensing a directed graph into a DAG of SCCs for further processing
Detecting cycles in directed graphs (any SCC of size > 1 contains a cycle)
gotchas: Only update low[u] via back-edges to nodes currently on the stack (on_stack[v] must be True)
When low[u] == disc[u], pop the stack until you pop u — those nodes form the SCC
Tarjan's outputs SCCs in reverse topological order of the condensation DAG

exercise.prompt: Complete Tarjan's SCC — update low[u] correctly for tree edges and back-edges.
exercise.starter_code:
import sys
sys.setrecursionlimit(10000)

def tarjans_scc(n, adj):
    disc     = [-1] * n
    low      = [0]  * n
    on_stack = [False] * n
    stack    = []
    sccs     = []
    timer    = [0]

    def dfs(u):
        disc[u] = low[u] = timer[0]
        timer[0] += 1
        stack.append(u)
        on_stack[u] = True

        for v in adj.get(u, []):
            if disc[v] == -1:
                dfs(v)
                low[u] = min(low[u], _____)   # tree edge: pull child's low up
            elif on_stack[v]:
                low[u] = min(low[u], _____)   # back-edge: reach back to disc[v]

        if low[u] == disc[u]:          # u is SCC root
            scc = []
            while True:
                w = stack.pop()
                on_stack[w] = False
                scc.append(w)
                if w == u: break
            sccs.append(sorted(scc))

    for u in range(n):
        if disc[u] == -1:
            dfs(u)
    return sccs
hints: Tree edge (u→v, v unvisited): after DFS(v), update low[u] = min(low[u], low[v]) — the child may reach further back
Back edge (u→v, v on stack): update low[u] = min(low[u], disc[v]) — v's discovery time is the earliest reachable point
exercise.test_code:
# Graph: 0->1->2->0 (SCC {0,1,2}), 1->3 (SCC {3})
adj = {0:[1], 1:[2,3], 2:[0], 3:[]}
sccs = tarjans_scc(4, adj)
assert len(sccs) == 2
assert [0,1,2] in sccs
assert [3] in sccs

# All separate
adj2 = {0:[1],1:[2],2:[]}
sccs2 = tarjans_scc(3, adj2)
assert len(sccs2) == 3
assert all(len(s)==1 for s in sccs2)
print('PASS')
